November 14, 2025 release 
1. Return to Publishing
Respiratory Illness Data Channel updates were published after a lapse in funding. Some types of data were omitted due to delays in data processing. The Respiratory Illnesses Data Channel website was not updated from October 3, 2025-November 13, 2025. 
2. Addition of Flu R t on Activity Levels Page 
What: 
Epidemic trend estimates (R t ) for influenza will be introduced at the state level, using the same methodology currently applied to COVID-19. These estimates will be displayed alongside existing metrics to provide a consistent, comparative view across pathogens. 
Why: 
Incorporating influenza into the R t framework enhances situational awareness and supports more informed public health decision-making at the state level. 
3. RESP-NET Updates for Influenza
What: 
Respiratory illness season has officially begun, therefore flu hospitalization data from the Respiratory Virus Hospitalization Surveillance Network (RESP-NET) is now being published again.
